data.content:
The build confirms it: the file `GeneratedInfo.cs` is generated successfully, but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't see it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile Include>` item for the generated file inside the target, and add `DependsOnTargets` to ensure proper ordering with `CoreCompile`.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output directory. However, the generated file is never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` after writing the file.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-870c3a121af741f59b5322a2fa5ffe60/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

toolArgs.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `<Compile>` item group. MSBuild's `CoreCompile` only compiles files listed in that group, so the generated file was silently ignored.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item inside the target so the generated file is picked up by the compiler.
